info.name: 03-AsyncResolution
info.description: Resolution axis: a PENDING Stripe refund settles late (poll /info?refreshRefunds), and voids on failure — both sides. Owns the PENDING->SETTLED (poll) and PENDING->VOIDED (poll-fail) transitions, plus the check that actual_refunded_amount is populated from the Stripe response, and an idempotency re-poll. The Case-2 role/split + invoice tuples are only GLANCED (owned by 01). Signed-webhook drive is deferred (HMAC secret is Passetto-encrypted); the poll exercises the same processRefundResult dispatcher.

exec: var envType = pm.environment.get('envType'); var msu = pm.environment.get('mockServerUrl') || ''; var url = pm.request.url.toString();
if (envType && envType !== 'Local' && msu && url.indexOf(msu) > -1) { if (pm.execution && typeof pm.execution.skipRequest === 'function') pm.execution.skipRequest(); }
// Dynamic refund amounts derived from the real ride's charged amount T (self-captured in THIS run's gate).
// rf1 = 0.5*cap (primary), rf2 = 0.25*cap (secondary partial), rf3 = 0.5*cap. Sized off the REAL RIDE_FARE
// component cap (rideFareCap, captured from fare_breakup_info) — NOT off T — so rf1 never spuriously trips
// the per-component cap on a ride with fees/toll/parking. Falls back to T until the gate sets rideFareCap.

item.name: Rider Auth
Rider OTP
Gate: latest COMPLETED booking for fixed rider (run 00 first)

exec: var b = completed.length ? completed[0] : (list.length ? list[0] : null);
pm.test('Run 00 first — fixed rider has a completed booking', function () {
pm.expect(b != null, 'no completed booking for the fixed rider; run 00 (FixtureStripeRide) first').to.eql(true);
if (b) { pm.environment.set('customer_bookingId', b.id); console.log('Gate: booking ' + b.id + ' status ' + b.status); }
var d = {}; try { d = pm.response.json(); } catch(e) {}
// the list handler can 500 transiently on an unrelated async notification fork — retry once before failing
pm.sendRequest({url: pm.request.url.toString(), method: 'GET', header: {token: pm.environment.get('rider_token')}}, function (err, res) {
var d2 = {}; try { d2 = res.json(); } catch(e) {}

item.name: Capture BAP ride id (SQL — poll until drained)

exec: if(!rows.length){ pm.test('BAP ride row drained (run 00 first)', function(){ pm.expect(0,'no ride for booking; run 00').to.be.above(0); }); return; }
// NOTE: r.id is the RIDE id. orderId is a SEPARATE payment_order GUID, captured next via domain_entity_id.

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{ mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody() } }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } else { _finish([]); } }); }

item.name: Capture payment order short id + intent (SQL — poll until drained)

exec: if(!rows.length){ pm.test('payment_order row present', function(){ pm.expect(0,'no payment_order for ride').to.be.above(0); }); return; }

pm.test('payment_order row present (orderId != rideId)', function(){ pm.expect(o.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(o.id, 'orderId must be a real GUID, not the rideId').to.not.eql(pm.environment.get('rideId')); });
pm.test('order has a Stripe payment intent (pi_)', function(){ pm.expect(/^pi_/.test(String(o.payment_service_order_id))).to.eql(true); });

item.name: PRECONDITION: seeded ride has commission > 0 (commission gate)

exec: // HARD precondition: 02's success path approves with deductFromDriver=true (Case-2), which exercises the
// commission slice (RideFareRefundCommission leg + negative Commission refund invoice). Those checks
// SKIP when bpp_commission==0, so a zero-commission ride makes them vacuously green. Fail loudly here instead.
pm.test('PRECONDITION: seeded ride MUST have commission > 0 (else the Case-2 commission asserts are vacuous; re-run 00 with a commissioned ride)', function () {

pm.expect(_C, 'bpp_commission is ' + pm.environment.get('bpp_commission') + ' -> the commission-slice path (RideFareRefundCommission leg + negative Commission invoice) cannot be exercised').to.be.above(0);

item.name: Wait for BAP fare invoice drained (refund-invoice source)

pm.test('BAP fare invoice drained (refund-invoice source)', function(){ pm.expect(rows.length).to.be.above(0); });

item.name: Capture RIDE_FARE component cap (SQL fare_breakup_info — refund cap source)

exec: // RIDE_FARE cap = sum of these tags (= li.rideFare + li.gstAmount, RidePayment.hs:841). TOLL/PARKING analogous (D1 uses tollCap/parkingCap).
function _sum(arr, tags){ return (arr||[]).filter(function(b){ return tags.indexOf(b.description) >= 0; }).reduce(function(a,b){ return a + Number(b.amount||0); }, 0); }
if (rows && rows.length) { try { bk = JSON.parse(rows[0].fare_breakups || '[]'); } catch(e) { bk = []; } }
console.log('Gate: rideFareCap='+rfCap+' tollCap='+tollCap+' parkingCap='+parkCap+' (from fare_breakup_info; refund amounts fall back to T if rideFareCap=0)');
pm.test('RIDE_FARE component cap captured from fare_breakup_info', function(){ pm.expect(rfCap, 'zero RIDE_FARE cap — stale fixture, re-run 00').to.be.above(0); });

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{ mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody() } }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } else { _finish([]); } }); }

exec: // 01-05 raise refunds as the customer via {{riderToken}}
pm.test('sanity: T captured and positive', function () { pm.expect(T).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test('sanity: all refund ids captured', function () {
console.log('Refund env ready. T =', T, pm.environment.get('txn_currency'), '| commission =', pm.environment.get('bpp_commission'));
// Refund amounts sized off the REAL RIDE_FARE component cap (rideFareCap from fare_breakup_info),
// falling back to T if the cap was not captured. rf1=0.5*cap, rf2=0.25*cap, rf3=0.5*cap.

item.name: Reset refund state for the ride (clean start)

exec: // SYNCHRONOUS single clear — the runner waits for THIS request, so no async re-fire can wipe a later-created refund_request.
pm.test('refund state reset (synchronous clean start)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Clear mock overrides (avoid leaked async state from an interrupted prior run)

exec: pm.test('mock overrides cleared (clean slate)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201,204]); });

item.name: Switch City for BAP (Rider Dashboard)

exec: pm.test('BAP dashboard switch OK', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Mock override: refund create -> pending
Raise Refund Request rf1 = T/2 (Customer)

exec: pm.test('raise accepted', function () { if (pm.response.code === 400 && /refundable cap/i.test(pm.response.text())) { pm.expect.fail('Raise rejected by the per-component cap: the RIDE_FARE T-fraction exceeds the RIDE_FARE component total (rideFare+gst). Reduce the T-fraction in the prerequest. Body: ' + pm.response.text()); } pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Approve (returns PENDING)

exec: pm.test('approve accepted (not FAILED)', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); var d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.status).to.not.eql('FAILED'); });

item.name: Assert BAP legs PENDING + capture re_

exec: function _log(rows){ try { console.log('  ↳ refund legs:', JSON.stringify(rows.filter(_isRefund).map(function(x){return {st:x.status, amt:x.amount, type:x.reference_type};}))); } catch(e){} }

function _assert(rows){ _log(rows); pm.test('B4 refund leg(s) PENDING after async approve', function(){ var pend = (rows||[]).filter(function(r){return r.status==='PENDING' && _isRefund(r);}); pm.expect(pend.length, 'expected PENDING provisional refund legs after async approve').to.be.above(0); }); }

else { pm.test('re_ captured', function(){ pm.expect(0,'refunds row did not drain / no re_ id after polling').to.be.above(0); }); }

item.name: Assert success: refund_request REFUNDED (positive)

exec: pm.test('async success: refund_request became REFUNDED (NOT trivially green)', function(){
pm.expect(rows[0].status, 'after the succeeded poll the request MUST be REFUNDED').to.eql('REFUNDED');
pm.expect(rows[0].refunds_id, 'refunds_id should be set on success').to.not.be.empty;

item.name: Assert success: refunds REFUND_SUCCESS + actual_refunded_amount (actual amount)

exec: var d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.count, 'no refunds row').to.be.above(0);
pm.expect(r.actual_refunded_amount != null, 'task i: actual_refunded_amount must be populated on success (not null)').to.be.true;
pm.test('actual_refunded_amount is populated from the Stripe response', function () {
var d = pm.response.json(); var r = (d.rows||[]).filter(function(x){return x.status==='REFUND_SUCCESS';})[0];
pm.expect(Number(r.actual_refunded_amount), 'actual_refunded_amount must be populated from the Stripe response').to.be.closeTo(requested, 0.02);

item.name: GLANCE: BAP refund invoice exists (negative) after settle — tuple owned by 01

exec: pm.test('GLANCE a negative Paid BAP refund invoice exists after settle (tuple owned by 01)', function(){ pm.expect(inv.length,'expected a Paid Refund invoice').to.be.above(0); pm.expect(Number(inv[0].total_amount)).to.be.below(0); });

item.name: GLANCE: BPP Commission refund invoice exists Draft (magnitude owned by 01)

exec: pm.test('GLANCE Case-2 Commission refund invoice exists Draft (magnitude owned by 01)', function(){ pm.expect(_C,'commission>0 (00 gates it)').to.be.above(0); pm.expect(comm.length,'expected a Commission refund invoice').to.be.above(0); pm.expect(comm[0].status).to.eql('Draft'); pm.expect(Number(comm[0].total_amount)).to.be.below(0); });

item.name: Idempotency: re-poll /info (re-fire the already-resolved refund)

exec: pm.test('re-poll ok (processRefundResult is a no-op when status is unchanged)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Idempotency: still exactly ONE Paid refund invoice (no double-settle)

exec: try{ console.log('  ↳ BAP Refund invoices after re-poll:', JSON.stringify(rows.map(function(x){return {st:x.status,type:x.invoice_type};}))); }catch(e){}

pm.test('Idempotent: re-poll did NOT mint a second refund invoice (exactly 1 Paid Refund)', function(){ pm.expect(paid.length,'exactly one Paid Refund invoice must exist after a redundant re-poll').to.eql(1); });

item.name: [AsyncFail] Mock override: refund create -> pending
[AsyncFail] Raise Refund rf2 = T/4 (Customer)

exec: pm.test('raise accepted', function () { if (pm.response.code === 400 && /refundable cap/i.test(pm.response.text())) { pm.expect.fail('Raise rejected by the per-component cap: the RIDE_FARE T-fraction exceeds the RIDE_FARE component total (rideFare+gst). Reduce the T-fraction in the prerequest. Body: ' + pm.response.text()); } pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: [AsyncFail] Capture 2nd refundRequestId (OPEN — after drain)

exec: function _finish(rows){ pm.environment.set('refundRequestId', rows[0].id); pm.test('2nd OPEN captured', function(){ pm.expect(rows.length,'expected an OPEN rf2 refund_request').to.be.above(0); }); }

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw',raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=(res.json().rows)||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ var _t=Date.now(); while(Date.now()-_t<1500){} _poll(n-1); } else { pm.test('2nd OPEN captured', function(){ pm.expect(0,'rf2 OPEN refund_request did not appear after polling').to.be.above(0); }); } }); }

item.name: [AsyncFail] Approve (returns PENDING)

exec: pm.test('approve accepted (not FAILED yet)', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); var d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.status).to.not.eql('FAILED'); });

item.name: [AsyncFail] Capture the new re_ (non-success refunds row)
[AsyncFail] Mock override: refund GET -> failed
[AsyncFail] Poll /info?refreshRefunds=true
[AsyncFail] Assert request FAILED after poll (SQL — after drain)

exec: function _log(rows){ try { console.log('  ↳ DB rows:', JSON.stringify(rows.map(function(x){return {st:x.status, amt:x.amount, total:x.total_amount, type:(x.reference_type||x.invoice_type||undefined)};}))); } catch(e){} }

function _assert(rows){ _log(rows); pm.test('GET->failed poll leaves the request FAILED', function(){ pm.expect(rows.length, 'refund_request not drained').to.be.above(0); pm.expect(rows[0].status, 'async GET->failed should set the request FAILED').to.eql('FAILED'); });  }

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }

item.name: [AsyncFail] Assert provisional legs VOIDED (SQL) + clear override

exec: function _log(rows){ try { console.log('  ↳ refund legs:', JSON.stringify(rows.filter(_isRefund).map(function(x){return {st:x.status, amt:x.amount, type:x.reference_type};}))); } catch(e){} }

function _assert(rows){ _log(rows); pm.test('[AsyncFail] BAP: async-failed refund VOIDED its provisional refund leg(s)', function(){ pm.expect((rows||[]).filter(function(r){return r.status==='VOIDED' && _isRefund(r);}).length, 'expected a VOIDED provisional refund leg').to.be.above(0); }); pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/override', method:'DELETE' }, function(){}); }

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }

item.name: [AsyncFail] Assert BPP provisional legs VOIDED too (SQL)

exec: try { console.log('  ↳ BPP refund legs:', JSON.stringify((rows||[]).filter(_isRefund).map(function(x){return {st:x.status, amt:x.amount, type:x.reference_type};}))); } catch(e){}
pm.test('[AsyncFail] BPP: provisional refund legs VOIDED on the FAILED branch', function(){

pm.expect((rows||[]).filter(function(r){return r.status==='VOIDED' && _isRefund(r);}).length, 'expected VOIDED BPP provisional refund legs (the BAP->BPP FAILED wire voids the rf2 legs)').to.be.above(0);

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody()} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(_ok(rows) || n<=0){ _assert(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }
